rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing annex blocks or fixing invalid references
Use only valid AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for const and eq declarations
generic_rules: Repair only the specific diagnostic issue without changing unrelated architecture
Use visible identifiers when available to maintain traceability
Ensure all guarantees have valid types and references
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate annex scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar types like integer or boolean
Do not leave unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations in guarantees
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics about missing EOF at 'annex' are not relevant to the current repair task
